items.headline: Global Equities Edge Higher Amid Geopolitical Hopes and Earnings, Caution Remains
items.summary: US stock futures pointed to a higher open, signaling Wall Street's intention to conclude the week on a positive note, fueled by optimism surrounding potential US-Iran peace talks and robust corporate earnings. This positive sentiment helped temper concerns over oil prices, which had seen hour-to-hour swings before easing. However, lingering uncertainties around these diplomatic efforts continue to cap overall market enthusiasm. European markets also experienced gains, notably in chip and AI-related stocks. In Asia, major indices advanced, with Japan's Nikkei 225 recording a strong rally.

items.headline: IBM Surges on Quantum Computing Grants
items.summary: IBM surged as it reportedly received the largest funding allocation from a $2 billion US government grant for the quantum computing sector.

items.headline: Workday Beats Earnings Estimates, Shares Soar
items.summary: Workday Inc. ($WDAY) saw its stock soar after reporting first-quarter earnings of $2.66 per share, exceeding the Street's estimate of $2.51. Quarterly revenue of $2.54 billion also beat the consensus of $2.52 billion.

items.headline: Ralph Lauren Jumps on Strong Quarterly Results
items.summary: Ralph Lauren surged following the release of stronger-than-expected quarterly financial results.

items.headline: Ross Stores Rises on Better-Than-Expected Q1
items.summary: Ross Stores Inc. ($ROST) rose in premarket trading after reporting better-than-expected first-quarter financial results and raising its full-year 2026 earnings guidance.

items.headline: Nvidia Dips Despite Strong Results
items.summary: Nvidia's shares fell despite reporting strong quarterly results, as market expectations reached 'Olympic-level high jump' levels.

items.headline: Walmart Trades Lower on Disappointing Outlook
items.summary: Walmart shares traded weaker today after the company's earnings outlook disappointed investors.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh Sworn In as New Federal Reserve Chairman
items.summary: Kevin Warsh officially assumed the role of Chairman of the Federal Reserve Board today, succeeding Jerome Powell. His appointment marks a significant transition during a period of macroeconomic complexities and elevated inflation.

items.headline: Markets Project Unchanged Fed Rates for June
items.summary: The CME Group's FedWatch tool indicates a 97.2% probability that the Federal Reserve will maintain its current interest rates during the upcoming June meeting. This comes as Nomura forecasts no Fed rate cuts in 2026, citing persistent inflation and reduced support for easing among Fed officials.

items.headline: US May Consumer Sentiment and Inflation Expectations in Focus
items.summary: Investor attention is on the final release of the US May University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment data today. The market consensus anticipates the index to remain at 48.2. A key focus will be on the 1-year and 5-year inflation expectations, which directly influence Federal Reserve policy projections.

items.headline: Richemont Reports Strong FY26 Sales and Profit Growth
items.summary: Luxury goods group Richemont delivered robust financial results for the year ended March 31, 2026. Sales increased by 5% at actual exchange rates to €22,420 million, or 11% at constant exchange rates. Operating profit rose by 1% (23% at constant rates) to €4,492 million, achieving a 20.0% operating margin.

items.headline: Energy Sector Outperforms as Tech Lags in 2026
items.summary: A notable market rotation continues in 2026, with the Energy sector vastly outperforming, gaining 21.5% year-to-date. In contrast, the Technology sector is down 3% year-to-date, indicating a shift from growth to more cyclical areas such as materials, which are up 17.6%. Morningstar suggests reallocating from growth to value stocks, as the undervaluation in technology has narrowed.

items.headline: Iran Peace Talk Hopes Buoy Sentiment, Oil Volatility Remains
items.summary: Hopes for a diplomatic resolution to the US-Iran conflict continue to support markets, easing oil worries and driving firmer risk appetite. However, persistent uncertainty over Iran's enriched uranium stockpile and the crucial Strait of Hormuz continues to cause volatility in crude oil prices.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Signals from Europe
items.summary: Germany's IFO Business Climate index rose to 84.9 points, surpassing expectations. Conversely, the UK private sector experienced a contraction in May, with the flash composite PMI falling to 48.5 from 52.6, marking its first contraction in over a year.
